description: Coherence registry (CO-0, ADR 0004). One entry per SHIPPED FR in docs/REQUIREMENTS.md — draft/future FRs are never registered (anti-sprawl bound B1). Edges: kind C = compatible, S = synergy, X = conflict; every X edge MUST carry a non-empty resolution_ref (G-FUNC-4 — an unresolved contradiction cannot merge). Guards (CO-1/CO-2, apps/web/src/__tests__/coherenceRegistry.test.ts once landed): G-FUNC-1 FR referential integrity, G-FUNC-2 policy-id registration, G-FUNC-3 invariant-id validity, G-FUNC-4 no unresolved X, G-FUNC-5 surface freshness (soft). Maintenance: an entry is added when its FR is adopted into REQUIREMENTS, extended when a slice adds a cross-feature interaction, removed only when the FR is retired.

interacts_with.note: Containment governs the online awaiting-parse path of this same surface.
Capture ubiquity extends this surface's reach (PWA/offline); raw-save-first is shared doctrine.
Provider canary + auto-degrade protect this parse path's availability.
Identity context (charters/operator profile) is injected through the NS-INV-1 context-assembly choke point.
Person/commitment proposals are approved at triage (propose-approve-persist).
Triage accept-to-today is a WIP-gated activation path: the 4th active item is refused, never silently queued.
Scheduling is gated: no time block commits without a sub-60s first move on the task.
Calendar-load-aware daily focus consumes free/busy to size the focus budget.
Re-entry auto-defer must never mutate an external calendar: Google-backed blocks are routed to approval, and the transition RPCs refuse Google-backed rows at the database layer.
interacts_with.resolution_ref: docs/REQUIREMENTS.md FR-028 acceptance criteria (no external calendar mutation)

interacts_with.note: Execute start is a WIP-gated activation path.
DoD-cap fires at execution session close: cut scope or defer, no third state.

Boundary between per-block Flow-8 recovery and batched re-entry amnesty: blocks that lapsed BEFORE the absence window stay in Flow 8; blocks whose end fell INSIDE the absence window are batch-deferred by the re-entry rule.
Wins harvest and rollup approval live in the review surface.
Learning consumer v1 reads these records to source recalibrations and policy-change proposals.
Persistence truth (mock_only/persisted) is surfaced here; the loud demo banner is the same signal made unmissable on write surfaces.
Provider-down and degrade/recovery states are visible on health.
New user-owned tables (people) inherit export coverage as an adoption precondition.
Waiting-on aging (S4) feeds the daily brief and side-rail surfaces.
The focus budget and the WIP cap are complementary limits on the committed set.

Re-entry auto-defer only REDUCES the committed count (frees WIP slots before the ritual renders), and the single recovery move follows the normal activation path — it inherits the WIP check and launch-sequence gate with no special-cased bypass.
interacts_with.resolution_ref: docs/REQUIREMENTS.md FR-028 acceptance criteria; plan-daily-driver-floor harmony matrix (C1-C4 row)

interacts_with.note: Both are activation-path gates; a swap-in passes the launch-sequence gate like any fresh commitment.

Same capture surface, complementary halves: FR-026 owns the online awaiting-parse containment path (return hook, no second capture, synchronous degrade); FR-027 owns the raw-save/offline/share path with no parse wait. Implementation lands as ONE packet set so return_hook and client_capture_id reach capture_items together; never both waiting at once.
interacts_with.resolution_ref: docs/REQUIREMENTS.md FR-027 (FR-026 reconciliation clauses); plan-daily-driver-floor §3 hot-spot resolution

interacts_with.note: The PWA is trusted first-party spine reach, not a perimeter channel: no tokens held, no new write path beyond the existing authenticated capture_items insert.

features.title: Re-Entry Amnesty (return without a guilt wall)

policies.note: Deterministic re-entry auto-deferral events (decided_by system, enumerated in the ritual).
WIP refusal/swap records. Registered ahead of code landing (C1 #364 in flight).
DoD-cap outcome records. Registered ahead of code landing (C4 #367 queued).
Mock workflow schema marker used by parser/workflow production source.
Parse-capture prompt contract version used by the AI context assembly path.
Rollup-prose enhancement prompt contract version used by the AI context assembly path (E3).
Task-map v1 AI graph draft suggestion/override policy id (slice 4: generation suggestion + per-node approve-time override diffs).
Task-map v1 draft prompt contract version used by the AI context assembly path (slice 4).
Deterministic closure-command policy core (issue #597 slice 1): complete/released terminal commands with the exact three-part summary; consumed by the later persistence/UI slices.
